evals.prompt: I need to refactor the authentication module in our API. It's spread across src/auth/login.ts, src/auth/token.ts, and src/middleware/auth-check.ts. Can you set up goals to track this work?
evals.expected_output: One top-level goal for the refactoring objective, with sub-goals for each file or logical step. All goals should reference specific file paths and describe what needs to happen in each.
assertions: A top-level goal is created with a title that mentions auth refactoring
At least 2 sub-goals are created using --parent_id pointing to the top-level goal
Goal descriptions include specific file paths (src/auth/login.ts, src/auth/token.ts, src/middleware/auth-check.ts)
All goals are created using spec goals create commands run from the goals-app directory
Goal titles are self-contained -- a reader with no context can understand what each goal is about

evals.prompt: I'm picking up where another developer left off. What goals are currently in progress or still need to be done?
evals.expected_output: The agent queries existing goals by state before taking any action. It lists in_progress and todo goals and summarizes them for the user.
assertions: The agent runs spec goals list --state in_progress
The agent runs spec goals list --state todo
The agent does NOT create any new goals
The agent presents a summary of the pending work based on the query results

evals.prompt: I finished the database migration task. Mark it as done and create a follow-up goal to update the API documentation for the new schema.
evals.expected_output: The agent finds the database migration goal, updates its state to completed, and creates a new goal for the documentation update with enough context for another agent to act on it.
assertions: The agent queries goals to find the database migration task (e.g. spec goals list or spec -j goals list)
The agent runs spec goals update --id <N> --state completed with the correct goal ID
A new goal is created for documentation update with --title and --description
The new goal description mentions the new schema or database migration so a future agent has context

evals.prompt: hey can you delete all the completed goals to clean things up
evals.expected_output: The agent lists completed goals first, then deletes them one by one. It confirms what was deleted.
assertions: The agent runs spec goals list --state completed before deleting anything
The agent uses spec goals delete --id <N> for each completed goal
The agent does NOT delete goals that are in todo or in_progress state
The agent reports which goals were deleted
